quick_summary: Supabase is the open-source Firebase alternative built on PostgreSQL, gaining rapid adoption for its real-time capabilities, Row Level Security, and auto-generated APIs. It's a hot topic in full-stack interviews, testing your ability to build secure, real-time applications with minimal backend code.

detailed_content.explanation:
Supabase is an open-source Firebase alternative built on PostgreSQL. Instead of proprietary NoSQL, you get a real relational database with Row Level Security (RLS), realtime subscriptions, and auto-generated REST APIs. The client library wraps PostgREST (for database access) and GoTrue (for auth) into a single, clean interface.

Think of Supabase as a managed PostgreSQL with superpowers: every table automatically gets REST endpoints, every row can have security policies, and changes stream to connected clients in real-time. You write SQL; Supabase handles the API layer, auth, and realtime.
detailed_content.example:
```javascript
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'

const supabase = createClient(
  'https://your-project.supabase.co',
  'your-anon-key'
)

// Sign up a user
const { data: authData, error: authError } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
  email: 'user@example.com',
  password: 'secure-password-123'
})

// Insert data (RLS policies apply automatically)
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('posts')
  .insert({ title: 'Hello World', user_id: authData.user.id })
  .select()

// Realtime subscription
const channel = supabase
  .channel('posts-changes')
  .on('postgres_changes',
    { event: 'INSERT', schema: 'public', table: 'posts' },
    (payload) => console.log('New post:', payload.new)
  )
  .subscribe()

// Query with filters
const { data: posts } = await supabase
  .from('posts')
  .select('*, profiles(display_name)')
  .eq('published', true)
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
  .limit(10)
```
detailed_content.key_takeaway: Supabase auto-generates REST APIs from your PostgreSQL schema — define tables and RLS policies in SQL, and the client library handles the rest.
detailed_content.try_it: Create a Supabase table for a todo app with user_id, title, and completed columns. Write RLS policies so users can only see and modify their own todos.

pro_tip: In interviews, emphasize Supabase's Row Level Security as the key differentiator — it moves authorization to the database layer, making it impossible to bypass via API. Discuss Edge Functions for custom serverless logic, realtime for collaborative features, and how Supabase compares to Firebase (SQL vs NoSQL, open-source vs proprietary).
